block_00: knowledge; worth its weight in gold
block_01: The correct use & implementation of fundamental analysis may make all the difference between a successful trade & an unsuccessful trade.
block_02: Let us take “gold” for example. It has long been considered a safe haven asset as throughout history it has been viewed as a store of value. It is essentially a currency that cannot be manipulated by the interest rate policies of any one government, & has traditionally been used as a hedge against inflation.
block_03: We often see the price of gold increase, when the world is faced with political uncertainty (e.g. Brexit / U.S. & China trade wars) because many investors believe this may affect the value of currency.
block_04: Over the past week we have learned that the UK passed a bill to block a “no deal Brexit” as well as what appears to be successful agreement, between the United States President Donald Trump, & Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping, to meet in June, in order to put an end to the “trade war” which has been going on since Trump came into office.
block_05: How do you think this could affect the price of gold?
block_06: The above chart shows that the price of gold has been on an upward trend since Trump was inaugurated in January 2017.
